name: hashcat
title: hashcat
summary: World's fastest and most advanced password recovery utility
description:
World's fastest and most advanced password recovery utility
Hashcat supports five unique modes of attack for over 300 highly-optimized
hashing algorithms. hashcat currently supports CPUs, GPUs, and other
hardware accelerators on Linux, and has facilities to help enable
distributed password cracking.
.
Examples of hashcat supported hashing algorithms are:
MD5, HMAC-MD5, SHA1, HMAC-SHA1, MySQL323, MySQL4.1/MySQL5, phpass,
MD5(Wordpress), MD5(phpBB3), MD5(Joomla), md5crypt, MD5(Unix),
FreeBSD MD5, Cisco-IOS, MD4, NTLM, Domain Cached Credentials (DCC),
MS Cache, SHA256, HMAC-SHA256, md5apr1, MD5(APR), Apache MD5, SHA512,
HMAC-SHA512, Cisco-PIX, Cisco-ASA, WPA/WPA2, Double MD5, bcrypt,
Blowfish(OpenBSD), MD5(Sun), Double SHA1, SHA-3(Keccak), Half MD5,
Password Safe SHA-256, IKE-PSK MD5, IKE-PSK SHA1,
NetNTLMv1-VANILLA/NetNTLMv1-ESS, NetNTLMv2, Cisco-IOS SHA256,
Android PIN, AIX {smd5}, AIX {ssha256}, AIX {ssha512}, AIX {ssha1},
GOST, GOST R 34, Fortigate (FortiOS), OS X v10.8+, GRUB 2, IPMI2, RAKP,
HMAC-SHA1, sha256crypt, SHA256(Unix), Drupal7, WBB3, scrypt, Cisco $8$,
Cisco $9$, Radmin2, Django (PBKDF2-SHA256), Cram MD5, SAP, iSSHA-1,
PrestaShop, PostgreSQL, Challenge-Response Authentication (MD5),
MySQL Challenge-Response, Authentication (SHA1),
SIP digest authentication (MD5), Plaintext, Joomla < 2.5.18, PostgreSQL,
osCommerce, xt:Commerce, Skype, nsldap, Netscape, LDAP, nsldaps,
SSHA-1(Base64), Oracle S: Type (Oracle 11+), SMF > v1.1, OS X v10.4,
v10.5, v10.6, EPi, Django (SHA-1), MSSQL(2000), MSSQL(2005),
PeopleSoft, EPiServer 6.x < v4, hMailServer, SSHA-512(Base64),
LDAP {SSHA512}, OS X v10.7, MSSQL(2012 & 2014), vBulletin < v3.8.5,
PHPS, vBulletin > v3.8.5, IPB2+, MyBB1.2+, Mediawiki B type,
WebEdition CMS, Redmine.
.
Hashcat offers multiple attack modes for obtaining effective and
complex coverage over a hash's keyspace. These modes are:
.
* Brute-Force attack
* Combinator attack
* Dictionary attack
* Fingerprint attack
* Hybrid attack
* Mask attack
* Permutation attack
* Rule-based attack
* Table-Lookup attack
* Toggle-Case attack
* PRINCE attack

arguments.raw: --hex-wordlist  Assume words in wordlist are given in hex

arguments.raw: -k  Single rule applied to each word from right wordlist

arguments.raw: --keep-guessing  Keep guessing the hash after it has been cracked

arguments.raw: --keyspace  Show keyspace base:mod values and quit

arguments.raw: --machine-readable  Display the status view in a machine-readable format

arguments.raw: --markov-threshold  Threshold X when to stop accepting new markov-chains

arguments.raw: --opencl-info  Show info about detected OpenCL platforms/devices

arguments.description: Disable the use of $HEX[] in output plains
arguments.raw: --outfile-autohex-disable  Disable the use of $HEX[] in output plains

arguments.raw: --progress-only  Return ideal progress step size and time to process

arguments.raw: --remove  Enable remove of hash once it is cracked

arguments.raw: --rule-left  Single rule applied to each word from left wordlist

arguments.description: Single rule applied to each word from right wordlist
arguments.raw: --rule-right  Single rule applied to each word from right wordlist

arguments.raw: --speed-only  Return expected speed of the attack, then quit

arguments.raw: --status  Enable automatic update of the status-screen

arguments.raw: --stdin-timeout-abort  Abort if there is no input from stdin for X seconds

arguments.description: Threshold X when to stop accepting new markov-chains
arguments.raw: -t  Threshold X when to stop accepting new markov-chains

arguments.description: Enable ignoring of usernames in hashfile (Recommended: also use --show)
arguments.raw: --username  Enable ignoring of usernames in hashfile (Recommended: also use --show)

arguments.description: Disable the conversion of $HEX[] from the wordlist
arguments.raw: --wordlist-autohex-disable  Disable the conversion of $HEX[] from the wordlist

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use hashcat because World's fastest and most advanced password recovery utility.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use hashcat as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hashcat [options] hashfile [mask|wordfiles|directories]` as documented upstream for hashcat.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair hashcat with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hashcat --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install hashcat. World's fastest and most advanced password recovery utility.

Install with: sudo apt install hashcat
